summary: Updated the repo root Makefile with build-system quality-of-life improvements ported mostly from the author's Super Monkey Ball decomp work. The main changes added quiet-by-default command execution with VERBOSE=1 opt-in, automatic C header dependency generation via .dep files, cleaner status messages for major build steps, direct host compilation of tools/elf2dol from tools/elf2dol.c, and SHA1 verification as part of the default target.

key_files.role: Only changed file; added verbose/quiet mode, header dependency generation, tool build recipe, default checksum verification, and clearer build recipes.

what_changed: Added VERBOSE ?= 0 and QUIET := @ so normal builds suppress full commands while `make VERBOSE=1` shows them.
Added DEP_FILES := $(O_FILES:.o=.dep) and `-include $(DEP_FILES)` so generated dependency files are consumed by make.
Changed include flag construction from a single hardcoded INCLUDES assignment to INCLUDE_DIRS, SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS, and an addprefix-based INCLUDES expression.
Added HOSTCC := cc and HOSTCFLAGS := -Wall -O3 -s for host-side tool compilation.
Changed the default target to build $(DOL) directly and then run `sha1sum -c $(TARGET).sha1`.
Added user-facing progress messages such as `Linking ELF`, `Assembling`, `Compiling`, `Converting`, and `Building tool` while hiding commands through $(QUIET).
Changed C object compilation to first run host preprocessor dependency generation: `$(HOSTCC) -E ... -MMD -MF $(@:.o=.dep) -MT $@ $< >/dev/null`, then run mwcc and postprocess.
Replaced the old phony `tools` submake dependency with a direct Makefile rule that builds `tools/elf2dol` from `tools/elf2dol.c`.
Changed clean to remove both `build` and `$(ELF2DOL)` instead of invoking `$(MAKE) -C tools clean`.
Added a pattern rule for `%.dol: %.elf $(ELF2DOL)` to convert ELF output to DOL output using the locally built elf2dol tool.
smart_moves: Using a separate host compiler preprocessor pass for `-MMD` dependency generation avoids relying on the Metrowerks compiler to emit make-compatible header dependencies.
Keeping dependency generation in the object compile rule means header edits automatically trigger rebuilds of all dependent C files, which the PR body explicitly calls out as a motivation.
The VERBOSE/QUIET pattern improves everyday readability without removing access to full commands when debugging build failures.
Factoring include paths into INCLUDE_DIRS and SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS makes the include search order easier to maintain than one long literal INCLUDES string.
Building elf2dol directly from source in the top-level Makefile reduces reliance on a separate tools submake for this specific required build artifact.
Putting SHA1 verification in the default target keeps correctness checking visible after normal builds instead of depending on a separate manual step.
Adding concise echo lines gives useful build progress even when command echoing is suppressed.
decomp_lessons: For decomp projects with many C translation units and evolving headers, automatic generated header dependencies are important; otherwise edits to shared headers can leave stale object files and misleading match results.
A quiet-by-default build can make compiler, linker, checksum, and matching failures easier to see, while a `VERBOSE=1` escape hatch preserves debuggability.
Host tooling needed by the build can be modeled as ordinary Makefile targets, allowing correct rebuilds when the tool source changes.
When using nonstandard or old target compilers, it can be practical to use the host compiler only for preprocessing/dependency scanning and keep the original compiler for code generation.
Checksum validation belongs in the normal build path for decompilation work because byte-identical output is the central success criterion.
naming_conventions: Make variables introduced or refactored here use all-caps underscore names: VERBOSE, QUIET, DEP_FILES, HOSTCC, HOSTCFLAGS, INCLUDE_DIRS, SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS.
Generated dependency files follow the object-file basename with a `.dep` suffix via `$(O_FILES:.o=.dep)` and `$(@:.o=.dep)`.
Host-side build flags are separated from target compiler flags using HOSTCFLAGS versus CFLAGS.
Include path groups distinguish source-local include directories from shared/system include directories with INCLUDE_DIRS and SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching changes were made; the matching-relevant tactic is build correctness: regenerate objects when included headers change.
The Makefile preserves the existing special case for `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/lb/lbvector.o` by continuing to append `-inline auto -fp_contract on` to CFLAGS.
The postprocess step remains after mwcc compilation, still using PROCFLAGS with `-fprologue-fixup=old_stack`.
The build still runs SHA1 verification against `$(TARGET).sha1`, now as part of the default DOL build flow.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body states the changes were mostly ported from the author's Super Monkey Ball decomp and highlights two intended behaviors: `make VERBOSE=1` for full command output and automatic header dependency rebuilds.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify DEP_FILES or generated .dep handling in the Makefile.
Find later PRs that change the VERBOSE/QUIET build output convention.
Find later PRs that touch tools/elf2dol build rules or reintroduce tools submake behavior.
Find build failures or fixes related to host compiler dependency generation with HOSTCC -E -MMD.
Find later Makefile changes involving INCLUDE_DIRS or SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS include path ordering.
